findings.description: The document uses multiple <main> elements, which is structurally unusual but does not misrepresent content. The heading hierarchy uses h1 for MEMORANDUM and h2 for sections I-IV, which is acceptable. However, multiple <main> tags in a single HTML document is technically invalid HTML.
findings.evidence: HTML contains at least 5 separate <main> elements, one per page section.

findings.description: The page header information (State of North Carolina, address, officials' names) appears in both the page-header div and is also present in the original page 1 content, but this is acceptable as it represents the letterhead which only appears once in the PDF.
findings.evidence: The header block with PAT MCCRORY, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART, S. JAY ZIMMERMAN appears once in the HTML page-header div, matching the PDF.

findings.description: In the Groundwater Assessments bullet under the written comments section, the last sentence is split across pages 4 and 5 of the PDF. In the HTML, this creates two separate list items - the continuation on page 5 starts as a new bullet without the bold label, which slightly disrupts readability but the text content is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 ends with 'One' and page 5 continues with 'comment suggested that capping the ash will not protect against seeps...'. HTML splits this into the end of one <ul> and the start of a new <ul> on the next page section.
